volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Casina (Section 2.2.187 - Section 2.3.253c)

sections.label: Section 2.2.187
sections.original: Hém , quid est ? díc idem ( nám pol hau sátis meo
sections.translation: Hah ! What is it ? Prithee , repeat that same again ; for , on my word , I don ’ t in my mind sufficiently comprehend your complaints .

sections.label: Section 2.2.189
sections.original: Vír me habet péssumis déspicatám modis ,
sections.translation: My husband has put slights upon me in a most unworthy manner ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.190
sections.original: néc mihi iús meum óptinendi optio est .
sections.translation: and I have not the advantage of enjoying my own rights .

sections.label: Section 2.2.191
sections.original: Míra sunt , véra si praédicas , nám viri
sections.translation: ’ Tis surprising , if you say the truth ; for husbands can scarce obtain from their wives what ’ s their own right .

sections.label: Section 2.2.193
sections.translation: Why , against my will , he demands a female servant of me , who belongs to myself , and was brought up at my own expense ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.195
sections.translation: for him to give to his bailiff . But he is in love with her himself .

sections.label: Section 2.2.196b
sections.translation: Pray , do hold your tongue .

sections.label: Section 2.2.197b
sections.translation: But here we may speak at present ; we are alone —

sections.label: Section 2.2.198b
sections.original: Ita est . unde éa tibi est ?
sections.translation: It is so . But whence did you get her ? For a good wife ought to have no property

sections.label: Section 2.2.200
sections.original: clám virum , ét quae habet , pártum ei haud cómmode est ,
sections.translation: unknown to her husband ; and she who has got any , it is not to her credit , for she must either have purloined it from her husband , or obtained it by unfaithfulness . Whatever is your own , all that I take to be your husband ’ s .

sections.label: Section 2.2.203
sections.original: Tu quidem advorsum tuam amicam omnia loqueris .
sections.translation: Surely , you ’ re saying all this out of opposition to your friend .

sections.label: Section 2.2.204_205
sections.original: Tace sís , stulta , et mi auscúlta . nolí sis tu illi advórsari ,
sections.translation: Do hold your tongue , will you , simpleton , and attend to me . Do you forbear to oppose him , will you . Let him love on ; that which he chooses let him do , so long as nothing ’ s denied you at home .

sections.label: Section 2.2.207_210
sections.original: Satin sána es ? nam tu quidem ádvorsus tuam istaéc rem loquere .
sections.translation: Are you quite in your senses ? For really , you are saying these things against your own interest .

sections.label: Section 2.2.207_210b
sections.translation: Silly creature , do you always take care and be on guard against this expression from your husband —

sections.label: Section 2.2.211b
sections.translation: What expression ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.211c
sections.translation: Woman ! out of doors with you ! [*]

sections.label: Section 2.2.212
sections.translation: ’ St ! be quiet .

sections.label: Section 2.2.212b
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.212c
sections.translation: Hush !

sections.label: Section 2.2.212d
sections.translation: Who is it that you see ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.212e
sections.translation: Why look , my husband ’ s coming ; go you in-doors . Make all haste ; be off , there ’ s a dear .

sections.label: Section 2.2.213b
sections.translation: You easily prevail ; I ’ m off .

sections.label: Section 2.2.214_215
sections.original: Mox mágis cum otium mi et tibi érit , igitur
sections.translation: At a future time , when you and I shall have more leisure , then I ’ ll talk to you . For the present , adieu !

sections.label: Section 2.2.216b
sections.translation: Adieu ! ( Goes into her house . CLEOSTRATA stands aside .)

sections.label: Section 2.3.217
sections.original: Omnibus rebus ego amorem credo et nitoribus nitidis antevenire ,
sections.translation: I do believe that love excels all things and delights that are exquisite . It is not possible for anything to be mentioned , that has more relish and more that ’ s delicious in it . Really , I do much wonder at the cooks , who employ sauces so many ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.220
sections.original: eos eo condimento uno non utier , omnibus quod praestat .
sections.translation: that they don ’ t employ this one seasoning , which excels them all . For where love shall be the seasoning , that I do believe will please every one ; nor can there be anything relishing or sweet , where love is not mixed with it . The gall which is bitter , that same it will make into honey ; a man from morose into one cheerful and pleasant . This conjecture do I form rather from myself at home than from anything I ’ ve heard ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.225
sections.original: qui quóm amo Casinam , mágis niteo , mundítiis munditiam ántideo :
sections.translation: who , since I ’ ve been in love with Casina , more than in my young days have excelled Neatness herself in neatness ; I give employment to all the perfumers ; wherever an unguent is excellent , I perfume myself , that I may please her . And I do please her , as I think . But inasmuch as she keeps living on , my wife ’ s a torment . ( Catches sight of his WIFE , and speaks in a low voice .) I espy her standing there in gloominess . This plaguy baggage must be addressed by me with civility . ( Going towards her .) My own wife and my delight , what are you about ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.229b
sections.translation: Get you gone , and keep your hand off !

sections.label: Section 2.3.230
sections.original: Heiá , mea Iuno , nón decet esse té tam tristem tuó Iovi .
sections.translation: O fie ! my Juno . You shouldn ’ t be so cross to your own Jupiter . Where art come now ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.231b
sections.translation: Let me alone . ( Moves as if going .)

sections.label: Section 2.3.231c
sections.translation: Do stay .

sections.label: Section 2.3.231d
sections.translation: I shan ’ t stay .

sections.label: Section 2.3.231e
sections.translation: I ’ troth , then I ’ ll follow you . ( Follows her .)

sections.label: Section 2.3.232
sections.translation: Prithee , are you in your senses ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.232b
sections.translation: In my senses , inasmuch as I love you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.233
sections.translation: I don ’ t want you to love me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.233b
sections.translation: You can ’ t have your way there .

sections.label: Section 2.3.233c
sections.translation: You plague me to death .

sections.label: Section 2.3.234
sections.translation: I only wish you spoke the truth .

sections.label: Section 2.3.234b
sections.translation: There I believe you . ( Moves on .)

sections.label: Section 2.3.235
sections.translation: Do look back , O my sweet one .

sections.label: Section 2.3.235b
sections.original: Nempe ita ut tú mihi es .
sections.translation: About as much , I suppose , as you are to me . Whence is this strong smell of perfumes , prithee ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.236b
sections.translation: O dear , I ’ m undone ; to my misfortune , I ’ m caught in the fact . Why delay to rub it off my head with my cloak ? ( Rubs his head with his cloak .) May good Mercury [*] confound you , you perfumer , who provided me with this .

sections.label: Section 2.3.239
sections.original: Ého tu nihili , cána culex , vix téneor quin quae décent te dicam ,
sections.translation: How now , you worthless grey gnat ! [*] I can hardly restrain myself from saying what you deserve .

sections.label: Section 2.3.240
sections.original: sénecta aetate únguentatus pér vias , ignáve , incedis ?
sections.translation: In your old age , good-for-nothing , are you walking along the streets reeking with perfumes ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.241
sections.original: Pól ego amico dédi cuidam operam , dum émit unguenta .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I lent my company to a certain friend of mine , while he was purchasing some perfumes .

sections.label: Section 2.3.241b
sections.translation: How readily he did trump that up . Are you ashamed of anything ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.242b
sections.translation: Of everything that you like .

sections.label: Section 2.3.242c
sections.translation: In what dens of iniquity have you been lying ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.243
sections.translation: I , in dens of iniquity ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.243b
sections.original: Scío plus quam tu me árbitrare .
sections.translation: I know more than you think I do .

sections.label: Section 2.3.243c
sections.translation: What is it that you know ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.244
sections.original: Té sene omniúm † senem néminem esse ignáviorem .
sections.translation: That not one among all the old men is more worthless than yourself , an old man .

sections.label: Section 2.3.245
sections.original: únde is , nihili ? ubí fuisti ? ubí ? úbi bibisti ?
sections.translation: Whence come you , good-for-nothing ? Where have you been ? In what den amusing yourself ? Where have you been drinking ? You are come , on my word ; look at his cloak , how it ’ s creased . ( Points at it .)

sections.label: Section 2.3.246b
sections.translation: May the Gods confound both me and yourself , if I this day have put a drop of wine into my mouth .

sections.label: Section 2.3.247b
sections.translation: Very well then ; just as you like :

sections.label: Section 2.3.248_250
sections.translation: drink , eat , and squander away your property !

sections.label: Section 2.3.248_250b
sections.original: Óhe , iam satis , uxór , comprime te , nímium tinnis ,
sections.translation: Hold , wife ; there ’ s now enough of it ; you din me too much . Do leave a little of your talk , that you may wrangle with me to-morrow . But what say you ? Have you by this time subdued your temper , so as to do that in preference which your husband wishes to be done , rather than strive against him ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.253b
sections.translation: About what matter are you speaking ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.253c
sections.translation: Do you ask me ? About the handmaid Casina — that she may be given in marriage to our bailiff ,
